behavior_summary: DECEIVER::look is an event handler invoked when an actor inspects (looks at) a Deceiver object.

On invocation it configures per-object/process info state (opcodes that set info and process-exclude are executed immediately before output — inferred from disassembly) and then causes the inspecting process to receive a short text 'bark' with the literal string "Deceiver ".

The handler constructs/pushes the string, pushes the interacting process PID (passed in BP+06h), calls Item::bark to present the text to that process, cleans up, runs an implies/pop sequence that sets the return/result value, then suspends and returns to signal the event was handled.
notable_control_flow: Entry as a process-event handler (execution_form: process) triggered by the U8P_DECEIVER::look/inspect event.
Performs a small sequence of engine opcodes that set object/process info and 'process exclude' immediately before producing output (these effects are inferred and labeled as such).
Calls an external routine (Item::bark) and then executes an implies/pop sequence to set/adjust the return value before a suspend/return.
No iteration or branching logic is present beyond the single bark call and the return-handling sequence — control flow is linear and short-circuited to produce the description.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the opcodes described as 'set info' and 'process exclude' in this context: what specific flags/visibility or future-processing behavior do they change for the Deceiver object?
Whether the small implies/pop return-value manipulation conveys a success/failure code with any downstream effect, or is purely an engine-required convention for event handlers.
If there are any conditional cases where the bark is suppressed (none observed in the disassembly excerpt), or whether other processes could intercept the bark.

main_function.name: U8P_DECEIVER::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a Look/Inspect action on a Deceiver object by presenting the label text to the inspecting process and marking/adjusting immediate object/process state prior to returning.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_DECEIVER::look): triggered when an actor uses the Look/Inspect action on this Deceiver object.
The inspecting process PID is passed in as the single parameter (at BP+06h).

function_map.name: U8P_DECEIVER::look
function_map.role: Handles the item's U8P_DECEIVER::look/inspect event; prepares state and invokes Item::bark to show the item's short label to the inspecting process.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API used to make a process/object emit/display a short string (the 'bark'). Called by DECEIVER::look to deliver the label.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver a short descriptive string ('Deceiver ') to the interacting process (PID supplied from BP+06h). Causes the target process to display/emit the message.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: DECEIVER::look is an event handler invoked when an actor inspects (looks at) a Deceiver object.
